document.name: repo-review
document.title: Repository Review (clone + summarize)
document.summary: Provision a sandbox, git clone the repo into /sandbox/repo, then run a coding agent that reads the tree and produces a concise project summary. The summary is persisted as a workflow_artifact (slot=primary) so it renders front-and-centre on the run-detail Overview tab.

repoUrl.title: Repository URL
repoUrl.description: HTTPS clone URL of a public repo (private repos require an auth shim).

branch.title: Branch
branch.description: Branch / tag / commit to check out after clone.

focus.title: Review focus (optional)
focus.description: Optional extra emphasis appended to the agent prompt, e.g. 'security risks' or 'how is auth wired'.

body.prompt:
${ "You are reviewing a repository in read-only mode. The repo is cloned at /sandbox/repo from " + .trigger.repoUrl + " (branch " + (.trigger.branch // "main") + "). DO NOT modify, create, or delete any files. Use list_files / read_file / execute_command (with read-only shell commands like ls, find, head, cat, grep) to explore.

Produce a concise project review in markdown with these sections:

1. **What is this?** — one-paragraph project purpose, based on README / package metadata.
2. **Architecture & main subsystems** — directories, services, runtime, build/test tooling. Cite concrete paths.
3. **How is it deployed or operated?** — CI, container, infra-as-code clues. If unclear, say so.
4. **Key docs to read first** — 3-5 files a new contributor should open, with one-line rationale each.
5. **Top risks & open questions** — highest-priority technical, operational, or security concerns. Be specific; cite files.

Keep the whole review under ~700 words. End the response with the literal marker `TASK COMPLETE` on its own line." + (if (.trigger.focus // "") != "" then "

ADDITIONAL FOCUS: " + .trigger.focus else "" end) }
